title: Author PROP-074 + ship dome-mirror agent — architectural separation of git→FUSE sync into a single-purpose scheduled agent. Removes Step 4c from workspace-sync (returns it to its original FUSE→git-only identity). Solves the 6-iteration silent-skip bug class by removing the abstraction that caused it.
background:
After 6 architectural iterations today (PROP-066 helper extraction → PROP-068 collapse-into-Step-4b → PROP-072 hoist-to-FIRST → PROP-072 Layer 2 self-attestation → contextual reframe to 'bidirectional FUSE↔git syncer' → PROP-073 stage-delta gating + detective layer) all failing to make workspace-sync reliably invoke Step 4c (~14% success rate post-PROP-073, with the agent pattern-matching the unfamiliar node invocation as decorative), operator has concluded the abstraction is wrong: workspace-sync's months-long established identity as a FUSE→git pusher is fundamentally incompatible with adding a git→FUSE half. Sonnet (and likely Haiku) filter the unfamiliar action out via pattern-recognition; even Opus 4.6 (operator is testing it in current run) may or may not fix this and would be cost-prohibitive permanently.

Architectural solution: extract the git→FUSE sync into its own scheduled agent, dome-mirror, with a SINGLE-ACTION prompt. When the only action in the prompt IS the helper invocation, pattern-matching can't filter it out. workspace-sync returns to its original unidirectional identity (FUSE→git only), which is the identity Sonnet/Haiku has been reliably executing for months.

Operator preferences pre-baked into this directive:
  - Agent name: dome-mirror (operator-picked)
  - Model: Haiku (mechanical single-action; cost matters at hourly cadence; if reliability requires Sonnet/Opus, document and flag for operator decision)
  - Cadence: hourly (matches workspace-sync; pair as complementary halves)
  - Helper script: REUSE monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js as-is (proven working when invoked, just couldn't be reliably invoked from workspace-sync). Optionally rename to monitor/scripts/dome-mirror-sync.js for clarity — tinker's call based on grep-radius (rename vs alias)
  - Removal from workspace-sync.md: Step 4c block (and PROP-066/068/072/073 in-block fixes that became moot once Step 4c moved) removed entirely. workspace-sync.md opening reframed BACK from 'bidirectional' to its original 'FUSE→git pusher' identity (consistent with what the agent actually does in this new architecture).
  - PROP-073 detective layer disposition: REMOVE from workspace-sync (no Step 4c there means no mis-titled-commit risk to detect). Keep the helper script intact (still used by dome-mirror).

Q1_agent_name.rationale: operator-picked, descriptive (mirrors git state into FUSE), single word fits the agent family.

Q2_model.rationale: Mechanical single-action prompt. Cost matters at hourly cadence. If reliability needs Sonnet or Opus, tinker should flag for operator decision rather than silently upgrading.

Q3_cadence.rationale: Matches workspace-sync cadence as complementary halves. Same staleness window.

Q4_scheduling_offset_vs_workspace_sync.answer: dome-mirror runs FIRST in the hour (e.g. :00), workspace-sync runs OFFSET later (e.g. :05 or :10)
Q4_scheduling_offset_vs_workspace_sync.rationale: dome-mirror updates FUSE from git → workspace-sync's smart_copy then compares fresh FUSE vs clone. If workspace-sync ran first with stale FUSE, smart_copy might revert recent git commits via the mtime-guard backfire. Tinker should propose exact offset minutes.

Q5_helper_script_disposition.answer: REUSE sync-workspace-step4c.js as-is for v1; OPTIONALLY rename to dome-mirror-sync.js in a follow-up if grep-radius shows few callers
Q5_helper_script_disposition.rationale: Helper script is proven correct when invoked. Renaming is cosmetic and creates rebase noise. Defer the rename decision.

Q6_workspace_sync_reframe_back.answer: YES — reframe workspace-sync.md opening back to its ORIGINAL unidirectional FUSE→git identity, since that's what it actually does in this architecture
Q6_workspace_sync_reframe_back.rationale: The 'bidirectional FUSE↔git syncer' opening I added at commit 143419813f was a hypothesis-test that failed. With Step 4c removed, the bidirectional framing becomes inaccurate. Restore the original opening (or simplify it; operator open to tinker's editorial judgment).

Q7_prop_073_detective_layer.answer: REMOVE from workspace-sync.md (stage-delta gating + AGENT_NOTES backstop + filename-ISO parse + write-path log)
Q7_prop_073_detective_layer.rationale: PROP-073 was scaffolding to detect mis-titled commits when workspace-sync tried to do Step 4c work. With Step 4c removed entirely, there's nothing to detect. Keep the helper script intact (dome-mirror uses it). Mark PROP-073 superseded-by-PROP-074 in lifecycle.

Q8_lifecycle_for_prop_066_068_072.answer: Mark PROP-066, PROP-068, PROP-072 as 'superseded by PROP-074 architectural separation' OR 'partially-superseded' (helper script from PROP-066 is preserved, in-prompt fixes from 068/072 are obsolete). Tinker's call on the exact framing — they remain historical records.
Q8_lifecycle_for_prop_066_068_072.rationale: Don't delete — preserve audit trail. The 6-iteration sequence is itself a valuable architectural lesson worth documenting in PROP-074's retrospective section.

operator_action_required_alongside_tinke: Operator will use mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task (or equivalent) to register the new dome-mirror task after tinker authors the prompt + SKILL.md content. Tinker should include explicit SKILL.md content recommendations + cron expression so operator can copy-paste during the create step.
requested_work: Step 1 — Author PROP-074 with design + rationale + retrospective on the 6-iteration sequence (PROP-066/068/072/073 + contextual reframe). Retrospective should capture the lesson: 'extending an agent's responsibilities beyond its established identity triggers pattern-rejection failure modes that are not addressable via prompt design alone'. Useful for future architectural decisions.

Step 2 — Author monitor/prompts/dome-mirror.md as a NEW prompt file. Single-action focus. Recommended opening: 'You are the dome-mirror agent. Your single job: run monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js to propagate any new commits from origin/main into the FUSE workspace. That is the entire job. Run the helper, commit + push any sentinel it writes, exit.' Include the PAT-source-enforcement prelude (same pattern as workspace-sync.md) + disk-pressure pre-flight + cleanup of own clone (if it needs one — see Q-OP-CLONE below). Suggest the actual prompt body NOT exceed ~150 lines so position-bias and pattern-recognition failures from long prompts don't recur.

Step 3 — CLARIFICATION-ON-CLONE: dome-mirror's helper invocation: does it need its own clone, or can it run from FUSE / a no-clone path? The helper sync-workspace-step4c.js reads origin/main HEAD via git fetch and writes to ${WORKSPACE} (FUSE). It may need git in some path. Tinker investigate + recommend. If clone needed, mirror workspace-sync's clone pattern (CLONE=${SESSION}/dome-mirror-clone) + same disk-pressure gates + same cleanup. If no clone, simpler.

Step 4 — Modify monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md: (a) reframe opening back to original unidirectional FUSE→git pusher identity (revert commit 143419813f's framing, but keep the rest of the prompt structure); (b) REMOVE the Step 4c invocation block entirely from Step 4 collapsed bash; (c) REMOVE PROP-073 stage-delta gating + AGENT_NOTES self-attestation + filename-ISO parse + write-path log (these were detective layer for the now-removed Step 4c work); (d) keep PROP-068's collapse architecture in place (cleanup inlined at end of Step 4b's bash block) — that part still works and prevents the orphan class; (e) keep the [CLONE-DEBUG] / [CLONE-LEAK] verification logging added earlier today — those are detective-only and remain valuable. Net workspace-sync.md change: smaller prompt, returns to its original responsibility shape.

Step 5 — Mark PROP-066 / PROP-068 / PROP-072 / PROP-073 status updates: PROP-066's helper script is PRESERVED (PROP-074's mechanism); the in-prompt invocation is removed. Recommend tinker mark each as 'partially-superseded-by-PROP-074' with a sub-field explaining what's preserved vs removed. PROP-067 (the misframed cross-session-sweep PROP from earlier today) remains 'superseded'. PROP-071 (FUSE-staleness audit) remains independent and pending operator review.

Step 6 — Operator coordination point: tinker should produce a markdown snippet for the operator to use when calling mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task. Recommended fields: taskId='dome-mirror', cronExpression (per Q4 — e.g. '0 */1 * * *' if hourly at top of hour), description (1-2 sentences), and the SKILL.md prompt content (which should mostly just be a wrapper that cats monitor/prompts/dome-mirror.md + executes, same shape as dome-workspace-sync's SKILL.md). Provide as a quoted block in the run report so operator can copy-paste.

Step 7 — Validate: after tinker's prompt + workspace-sync.md changes commit, run a SYNTHETIC test by manually invoking the new dome-mirror.md prompt's procedure (without actually starting the new scheduled task — just verify the prompt's bash blocks parse, the helper invocation works, the sentinel gets committed). Operator will create the scheduled task after this validation.

Step 8 — Commit + push as ONE batched commit: new monitor/prompts/dome-mirror.md + modified monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md + modified PROP-066/068/072/073/074 lifecycle fields + new PROP-074 file + synthetic test result sentinel.

Step 9 — FUSE bootstrap-sync workspace-sync.md and dome-mirror.md to FUSE so the next scheduled workspace-sync cycle picks up the simplified prompt (per the per-session FUSE-staleness pattern documented in D31-010).

Step 10 — Lifecycle: mark this directive completed with prop_id_authored='PROP-074' AND prop_id_implemented='PROP-074' (tinker self-implements per pre-baked operator answers above). Post-implementation note: the new dome-mirror agent isn't actually running until operator creates the scheduled task (Step 6 deliverable provides the recipe).
non_goals: Do NOT delete the sync-workspace-step4c.js helper script — it's PROP-074's mechanism. Reuse as-is. Rename optional follow-up only.
Do NOT delete PROP-066/068/072/073 proposal files — preserve as historical record. Mark partially-superseded.
Do NOT change PROP-071 (FUSE-staleness audit) or any of its 36-site recommendations — independent work, operator review pending.
Do NOT bump cadences of other agents.
Do NOT touch the analyst HNOTE for ISS-2341/2342 (commit ed1603a7ed) — unrelated.
Do NOT create the scheduled task itself — that's operator's mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task call after tinker provides the recipe (Step 6).

Do NOT bump dome-mirror's model above Haiku without operator approval — Q-OP-2 default is Haiku. If tinker has strong reason to think Haiku will fail, flag and stop for operator decision rather than silently upgrading.

context_pointers.6_iteration_history: PROP-066 (commit ba212cf) → PROP-068 (e22b0e8) → PROP-072 (4177f601dc) → contextual reframe (143419813f) → PROP-073 (15674330) → all failed to make workspace-sync invoke Step 4c reliably. Sentinel rate post-PROP-073: 1/7 overnight cycles = 14%.
context_pointers.the_smoking_gun_commit: fb52feb8 (21:20Z 2026-05-31) — titled 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel' but contained 13 unrelated files. Proved the pattern-rejection failure mode.
context_pointers.current_workspace_sync_state: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md — bidirectional framing at top, collapsed Step 4 block with 4c at FIRST position, PROP-073 stage-delta gating + AGENT_NOTES self-attestation. All to be reverted/removed per requested_work Steps 4 + 7.
context_pointers.helper_script_to_preserve: monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js — works correctly when invoked. PROP-074 = invoke it from a single-purpose agent.
context_pointers.operator_opus_test_in_flight: Operator flipped current workspace-sync run to Opus 4.6 as an experiment (~05:50Z). Results pending ~06:00Z cycle. If Opus succeeds where Sonnet failed under identical conditions, that's evidence of model-capability-bound bug. dome-mirror architectural separation remains correct regardless because (a) Opus permanently is cost-prohibitive at hourly cadence and (b) single-purpose agents are more robust than multi-purpose agents to model-substitution.
context_pointers.scheduled_tasks_existing_pattern: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md + /sessions/<id>/mnt/.scheduled/dome-workspace-sync/SKILL.md show the wrapper pattern. dome-mirror should mirror.
lessons_to_capture_in_prop_074_retrospec: agent self-conception filters out unfamiliar actions regardless of prompt design
FIRST + LAST positions execute reliably across Sonnet; MIDDLE positions drop
self-attestation backstops are gameable (agent doesn't notice it didn't run)
stage-delta detection (PROP-073 #1) DID work for preventing mis-titled commits — defense-in-depth detection layers do work; preventive prompt-design fixes for pattern-rejection do not
architectural separation into single-purpose agents is the structural fix; extension of multi-purpose agents triggers the bug class

expected_output: Single commit on origin/main containing: monitor/prompts/dome-mirror.md (new) + monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (Step 4c removed, bidirectional framing reverted, PROP-073 detective layer removed) + monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-074-architectural-separation-dome-mirror.json (new, status='implemented') + lifecycle updates on PROP-066/068/072/073. PLUS: this directive flipped to status='completed' with prop_id_authored AND prop_id_implemented='PROP-074'. PLUS: operator copy-paste recipe for mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task in tinker's run report.
estimated_effort: ~60-90 min Opus. dome-mirror.md prompt is small (~100-150 lines target). workspace-sync.md edit is mostly DELETES (Step 4c block + PROP-073 instrumentation + contextual reframe). PROP-074 authoring is the bulk: design + retrospective + lifecycle updates + synthetic test.
priority_note: HIGH — operator is testing Opus 4.6 in current workspace-sync run; result expected ~06:00Z. Regardless of Opus result, architectural separation is the right move because (a) Opus permanently is cost-prohibitive at hourly cadence, (b) single-purpose agents are robust to model substitution and pattern-rejection failures, (c) 6 iterations of preventive prompt-design fixes have failed. Worth shipping the architectural fix before any further point-fixes accumulate.
